name: Connection Setup & Management
description: Sub-sector template for connection establishment/release, dual connectivity setup, carrier aggregation signaling, RRC state transitions, and NAS procedures patents.
contextDescription: Connection management covers the RRC procedures that establish, maintain, and release radio connections. In 5G NR, the RRC_INACTIVE state (Rel-15) enables fast resume without full connection setup. Dual connectivity (EN-DC for NSA 5G, NR-DC for SA) requires managing multiple cell groups simultaneously. Carrier aggregation SCell activation/deactivation is signaled via MAC-CE. NAS procedures (registration, service request) interface with RRC. 3GPP TS 38.331 defines NR RRC procedures; TS 36.331 for LTE. Every device connection starts with RRC setup — these patents have universal applicability.
scoringGuidance: RRC setup/release is exercised by every device every time it connects — massive infringement surface.
Dual connectivity management patents are critical: EN-DC is the dominant 5G deployment mode.
RRC_INACTIVE state patents are high-value for 5G NR — reduces signaling and connection latency.
SCell activation/deactivation patents target carrier aggregation — exercised in every CA-capable device.
Signaling overhead reduction patents improve network efficiency and user experience.

questions.question: How does this patent improve connection establishment? Score 8-10 for fast setup for dual connectivity, conditional configuration, or early data transmission. 5-7 for improved RRC signaling procedures or setup optimization. 3-4 for minor setup improvements. 1-2 for standard setup procedure with no innovation.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Connection setup delay directly impacts user experience. Fast setup techniques are high-value.

questions.question: Does this patent manage EN-DC, NR-DC, or multi-connectivity bearer handling? Score 8-10 for dynamic secondary cell group (SCG) management, split bearer optimization, or MCG/SCG coordination. 5-7 for basic DC setup/release procedures. 3-4 for DC awareness without specific management innovation. 1-2 for single connectivity only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: EN-DC is how most 5G devices connect today. SCG management is exercised continuously during 5G sessions.

questions.question: Does this patent optimize SCell activation/deactivation or CA configuration signaling? Score 8-10 for dynamic CA management, cross-carrier scheduling setup, or novel SCell procedures. 5-7 for basic SCell activation/deactivation improvements. 3-4 for CA mentioned without signaling innovation. 1-2 for no CA relevance.
questions.reasoningPrompt: SCell activation via MAC-CE is exercised by every CA-capable device — standard signaling with broad infringement.

questions.question: Does this patent optimize RRC state transitions (Connected, Inactive, Idle)? Score 8-10 for novel RRC_INACTIVE usage, fast state transitions, or suspend/resume optimization. 5-7 for improved transition procedures or timer management. 3-4 for basic state management. 1-2 for standard behavior with no optimization.
questions.reasoningPrompt: RRC_INACTIVE is new in 5G NR and enables fast resume — patents here are forward-looking and valuable.

questions.question: How much does this patent reduce NAS/RRC signaling overhead? Score 8-10 for significant reduction via piggybacking, early data transmission, or consolidated signaling. 5-7 for moderate signaling optimization. 3-4 for minor overhead awareness. 1-2 for no signaling overhead innovation.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Signaling overhead reduction improves network capacity — particularly valuable for massive IoT scenarios.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP RRC procedures (TS 38.331, TS 36.331)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of mandatory UE/gNB RRC behavior. 5-7 for enabling standard compliance or extending standard procedures. 3-4 for standard-adjacent approaches. 1-2 for proprietary connection management.
questions.reasoningPrompt: RRC procedures are mandatory per 3GPP — identify specific IEs, messages, and procedures.

questions.question: Is this connection management behavior observable in signaling traces? Score 8-10 for behavior visible in RRC messages (RRCSetup, RRCReconfiguration, RRCResume). 5-7 for behavior inferable from connection timing or conformance testing. 3-4 for internal UE/gNB processing. 1-2 for algorithm-level logic only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: RRC messages are fully logged in protocol captures — strongest evidence basis for infringement.
